summary_en: Kamgras 1 (offeror) filed corrective tender offer disclosure for Kakaku.com (2371, TSE Prime) following receipt of Australian ACCC clearance on 2026-05-25. Tender offer commenced 2026-05-13. Corrective filing addresses regulatory clearance condition satisfaction; no material change to offer terms per Article 27-3(2)(1) of Financial Instruments and Exchange Act.
title_en: Notice Regarding Correction of Public Tender Offer Commencement Announcement in Connection with Submission of Corrected Public Tender Offer Filing by Kamgras 1 Co., Ltd.
translation_en_brief:
On 2026-05-27, Kamgras 1 Co., Ltd. (representative: Robert Patrick Ryan) filed a corrective tender offer disclosure with the Kanto Financial Bureau regarding its ongoing public tender offer for all ordinary shares and share acquisition rights of Kakaku.com Inc. (Code 2371, TSE Prime Market). The tender offer commenced on 2026-05-13.

The correction was necessitated by Kamgras 1's receipt of a clearance decision from the Australian Competition and Consumer Commission (ACCC) on 2026-05-25 (local time), determining that prior notification was not required for the transaction. The filing updates conditions regarding potential withdrawal of the tender offer, specifically clarifying regulatory approval thresholds and related disclosure obligations. The correction does not constitute a material change to offer terms under Article 27-3(2)(1) of the Financial Instruments and Exchange Act.

The corrected disclosure addresses withdrawal conditions tied to specified financial covenants, including thresholds for extraordinary dividends and treasury share acquisitions set at 10% of net assets per the most recent balance sheet (approximately ¥6,131 million), and conditions related to prior false or omitted material disclosures by the target or its significant subsidiaries.
